engine_recommendations.alternative_for_near: If multiply blend introduces unwanted darkening, run a one-shot Pillow color-key script (PIL.Image: convert near-white pixels to alpha=0) instead of BiRefNet. Cheaper and more controllable for landscape framing layers.

sprints.sprint: Sprint 7A — Multi-frame Cosmo via Flux Control LoRA Canny
sprints.strategy: Programmatic stick-figure skeletons (PIL) → Flux Control LoRA Canny → BiRefNet
sprints.goal: 6 distinct pose-textures wired to Cosmo state-machine (idle/run/jump/fall/cling/damage)
pipeline: 1. Generate 6 stick-figure skeletons in Python/PIL with thick black lines + BIG black discs at hand-tips (signature suction-cup constraint)
2. Upload all 6 skeletons + canonical to fal.ai storage

3. For each pose: fal-ai/flux-control-lora-canny with skeleton as control_lora_image_url, NO image_url (canonical drags pose back to neutral — Sprint 5B learning), control_lora_strength=1.2, style-first prompt
5. Wire textures in L1Scene.preload + Cosmo.updateAnim() texture-swap state machine

side-view, both suction-cups extending right toward virtual wall

case_study_artifacts: raw-walk-1.png (v1, with canonical image_url — FAILED, pose lost, kawaii eyes, tail back)
raw-walk-1-v2.png (skeleton-only, strength 1.2 — pose held but anemic line-art style)
raw-walk-1-v3.png / raw-walk-1-FINAL.png (skeleton-only, strength 1.2, style-first prompt — PRODUCTION)
scripts: scripts/sprint7a/cosmo_controlnet.py — fal.ai pipeline (modes: test, test2, batch, all, redo-walk2)
wiring_changes: src/phaser/scenes/L1Scene.ts:156 — Cosmo init texture changed to 'cosmo-walk-1'
src/phaser/scenes/L1Scene.ts — removed redundant swapCosmoTexture() (Cosmo.updateAnim handles it)
src/phaser/entities/Cosmo.ts — replaced playStateAnim with updateAnim() texture-swap state machine
src/phaser/entities/Cosmo.ts — added walkPhase + walkFrameToggle for ~133ms walk-cycle alternation
key_learnings: Flux Control LoRA Canny WITHOUT image_url + skeleton-only is THE working pose-control pipeline. Passing canonical as image_url drags the result back to canonical neutral-stand pose — confirms Sprint 5B finding that image-to-image is a noise-init not skeletal-anchor

control_lora_strength sweet spot is 1.2 — at 0.85 the skeleton barely matters, at 1.0 the prompt-style wins and pose collapses to A-pose, at 1.2 pose holds and prompt still gets enough weight for character DNA
Programmatic stick-figures with BIG black discs at hand-tips force the Flux model to render JET-BLACK suction-cup-pads — it's not a sample-bias problem, it's a CONTROL problem solved by canny edge
Style-first prompt ordering (Studio Ghibli watercolor cues FIRST, character DNA SECOND) preserves more painterly look at high control_lora_strength
Inter-frame eye-style consistency is fragile (some frames get 1 ink-eye, others get 2 black-disc-eyes) — at 120px in-game this is acceptable, but a per-frame seed-lock could improve
Queue wait times for control-lora-canny can hit 100-150s during peak — bump submit_and_poll deadline to 600s
open_issues: Inter-frame style/eye drift: walk-1 has 1 ink-eye, walk-2 has 2 black-disc-eyes, hurt has 1 ink-eye + blush spots. Not catastrophic but visible if you compare side-by-side. A future pass could fix walk-2 with a different seed

Cling-right uses setFlipX for left-wall cling — works but the suction-cups will appear on the right side of the body when flipped (since body is asymmetric). Acceptable since both arms reach toward the wall in the source pose
Hurt-frame is reused for death state with no rotation — if death looks weird in playtesting, add a Z-rotation tween in Cosmo.takeDamage()

sprints.sprint: Sprint 11A — Cosmo multi-frame HIGH-FIDELITY body-mass re-render
sprints.strategy: img2img + ControlNet (NOT skeleton-only) — use canonical-v2-cleaned as image_url to preserve Hayao body mass while skeleton drives pose anchor
sprints.post_processing: PIL alpha-erase tail (Sprint 6A pattern) + fal-ai/birefnet/v2 (transparent PNG)
sprints.user_complaint: online versie heeft een soort stick puppet — Cosmo en enemies mogen gerust wat massaler en voller aanvoelen (Richard, 2026-05-01)

walk-1.pose_120px_distinct: PARTIAL — arms-down stance, but legs only marginally shifted from canonical idle

walk-2.pose_120px_distinct: PARTIAL — mirror-arm intent visible, leg-stride near-canonical

jump-up.pose_120px_distinct: FAIL — ghosted skeleton arms visible in halo, but rendered character keeps neutral idle pose

cling-right.pose_120px_distinct: FAIL — not sideways, character still upright

hurt.pose_120px_distinct: FAIL — no head-tilt or knockback splay

key_learnings_sprint_11a: BODY-MASS RESTORATION SUCCEEDED: img2img with canonical-v2-cleaned at strength 0.45-0.65 + control_lora_strength 1.20-1.25 PRESERVES Hayao watercolor body, chameleon eyes, faded-rose spots, pearl-drop head, suction-cup discs across all 6 frames. Stick-puppet complaint is FIXED.

POSE-SHIFT FAILED across all tested params: img2img + ControlNet + canonical-init has a hard architectural ceiling — the canonical pose-bias dominates regardless of img2img strength (tested 0.40, 0.45, 0.50, 0.55, 0.60, 0.65, 0.80) or control_lora_strength (tested 0.85-1.30). The skeleton appears as faint background ghost (visible in raw renders) but never replaces the canonical's neutral standing pose. CFG conditioning + canonical noise-init reverts deterministically to canonical pose.

ARCHITECTURAL FINDING: pose-variation REQUIRES skeleton-only mode (NO image_url) — confirms Sprint 7A finding. Sprint 11A's combined pipeline gives body+watercolor but loses pose; Sprint 7A's skeleton-only gave pose but lost body. The two are at odds in flux-control-lora-canny architecture. Future pose-variation work needs: (a) skeleton-only with thicker/fuller skeleton silhouettes that suggest body-volume so canny preserves mass, (b) sketch-to-img with hand-painted character-thumbnails per pose, or (c) train a Cosmo-specific LoRA so the model has the character internalized and skeleton drives pose without image_url.

Tail-recurrence: img2img with canonical re-introduces the tail (canonical's tail was alpha-erased in v0.5.1, but Flux 'fixes' the missing geometry). Solution: alpha-erase tail post-BiRefNet using PIL polygon mask + Gaussian feather (Sprint 6A pattern, 0% failure rate, $0 cost). All 6 final frames have tail erased deterministically.

Skeleton-design tip: BIG round JET-BLACK filled discs at hand-tips force suction-cup rendering even at low control-strength (Sprint 7A finding holds). New v11a skeletons drew hands with disc_radius=70px which produced large, jet-black suction-cup discs in all renders. Confirmed reusable pattern.
BiRefNet on dark-bg renders: works fine, isolates Cosmo cleanly even from solid black background. No need to enforce white-bg in prompt for transparency-extraction quality.

cost_breakdown.phase1_strength_sweep_walk1_old_skel: ~$0.27 (3 attempts at 0.40/0.50/0.60 with old skeleton — all near-identical, low pose-shift)
cost_breakdown.phase1b_control_strength_sweep: ~$0.27 (3 attempts at cls 0.95/1.05/1.15 — same neutral pose)
cost_breakdown.phase2_decisive_walk1_new_skel: ~$0.27 (3 attempts with v11a skeleton — confirmed pose-bias ceiling)
cost_breakdown.phase4_extreme_pose_retry: ~$0.27 (jump-up/jump-fall/cling-right at strength 0.80 — NO improvement over phase3)
cost_breakdown.total: ~$1.92 (within $5-8 budget; ample headroom remained but architectural ceiling reached — further iteration would not help)

open_issues: Pose-distinction at 120px display: walk-1/walk-2 have minor arm-angle variation but legs and torso are near-identical to idle; jump-up/jump-fall/cling-right/hurt do NOT visually read as their named poses. Game mechanics may need to compensate via tween/rotation/scale on top of texture swap. RECOMMENDED: Cosmo.ts updateAnim() should add rotation tween for jump-fall/hurt (Z-axis spin), scale-pulse for jump-up (squash+stretch), setRotation+setFlipX for cling-right.
Tail-stub remnants: BiRefNet sometimes preserves a thin vertical pixel-row at the feet from where the tail was alpha-erased. Cosmetic, invisible at 120px, no fix needed.

Sprint 11A confirms Sprint 5B+7A architectural finding: Flux text-conditional + canonical img2img cannot escape canonical-pose-bias. For TRUE pose variation, either (a) train a Cosmo LoRA from the existing renders + use skeleton-only inference, (b) hand-painted sketch-to-img per pose, or (c) use a 3D rigged Cosmo via Meshy and render poses from 3D. All three are larger scope than this sprint.

sprints.strategy: Quality > quantity. Multi-model selection per use-case. Img2img regen for character DNA preservation. Alpha-erase post-process for unbreakable Flux sample-bias.
phases_run: p1 cosmo hero (recraft attempt + flux-pro-ultra fallback + img2img regen from canonical-v2)
p3 8 cosmic bubbles parallel (flux-pro v1.1 + birefnet)
p4 6 hallucination particles parallel (flux dev + birefnet)
p5 share-card + 2 splash + 5 promo keyframes (mixed recraft/flux-pro-ultra/flux-pro)
p6 regens (cosmo img2img, slow-bloom + cathedral retry)
p7 final fixes (alpha-erase figure, alpha-erase tail, cathedral on flux-dev)

wow_ratings.biome-cathedral-4k: 9/10 (flux-dev regen produced real Hayao watercolor, 3 cosmic particles drifting, paper-grain visible)
wow_ratings.bubbles 1-8: 9/10 each (8/8 painterly watercolor orbs, transparent BG, distinctive palette per variant)
wow_ratings.card-frame-template: 7/10 (border-frame design B+, COSMOS watermark elegant, orange-field overpowers slightly)
wow_ratings.splash-hero portrait+landscape: 6/10 (kawaii-eye + finger-hand DNA-drift, but acceptable as promo art)
wow_ratings.promo keyframes 1-5: 6-9/10 (keyframe-3 deep-trip 9/10, keyframe-4 kaleido-burst 8/10, others 6/10 due to kawaii-drift)

regen_count.cosmo-hero: 2 (recraft 422 fail + flux-pro-ultra 1024×768 black-output, then img2img from canonical-v2 SHIP)
regen_count.slow-bloom: 3 (flux-pro-ultra v1 with figure, flux-pro-ultra v2 with 2 figures, alpha-erase, restored to v1, alpha-erase 1 figure remaining)
regen_count.cathedral: 2 (flux-pro-ultra v1 too photoreal, flux-dev v2 SHIP - real watercolor)

blockers_encountered: Recraft V3 returned HTTP 422 on response fetch (transient or unsupported style param) — fell back to flux-pro-ultra
Flux Pro v1.1 Ultra palette-collapse-to-black bug hit on Cosmo hero (pure-black output despite 'luminous bright pastel' rider)
Flux Pro v1.1 Ultra returned 1024x768 instead of requested 2048x2048 (aspect ratio interpretation)
BiRefNet over-stripped Cosmo hero raw (10KB → 3KB) — hero needed img2img regen via canonical-v2 with paper-grain emphasis
Flux 'lone wanderer in alien world' sample-bias unbreakable in mushroom-forest scenes (3 attempts, 8-fold negative not enough)
Flux Pro v1.1 forces photoreal aesthetic for sky/cloud scenes — Flux Dev pliabler for stylized watercolor (cathedral lesson)
Img2img from canonical regenerates the alpha-erased tail (Sprint 11A pattern confirmed); requires post-img2img alpha-erase
Splash + promo Cosmo's cannot avoid kawaii-drift via text-only — would need img2img-from-canonical (sacrifices pose variation, defeating promo purpose)
memory_lessons_added: Recraft V3 occasionally returns 422 on response endpoint — always have flux-pro fallback
Flux Pro Ultra still has palette-collapse-to-black bug — anti-dark rider must be EARLY in prompt, not just middle
Flux Pro Ultra interprets 'aspect_ratio: 1:1' but defaults to 1024 base unless given explicit width/height (which then forces v1.1 endpoint)
Cathedral/sky scenes: Flux Dev > Flux Pro for watercolor DNA (Pro forces photoreal)
Alpha-erase patch sources can introduce NEW Flux sample-bias artifacts (lone wanderer was IN the patch source too) — pick patch from far away from any path/people zones
Img2img from canonical at strength=0.45 with explicit DNA prompt PRESERVES Cosmo body+head+spots correctly but ADDS kawaii eye-drift (eyelashes, doll-eyes) — unavoidable in flux/dev/image-to-image
Final tail-erase polygon must extend further down than expected (canonical-v2 tail had ~120px stub below feet)
8-fold anti-character stack still insufficient for mushroom-forest scenes — wanderer-bias is essentially unbreakable for that biome

sprints.strategy: WEIRDO-energy: oneirisch, ongemakkelijk-fascinerend. Quality > quantity. Hayao×Moebius watercolor coherent met biomes + Cosmo. Per object 1-3 attempts; switch Flux Pro Ultra → Flux Dev voor stylized illustration when Pro forced photoreal aesthetic (Sprint 11C trampoline pattern confirmed). Mouth-pillar sprite-sheet via 4-separate-frames + PIL composite (deterministic alternative to single-image sprite-sheet which suffers from symmetry-bias).
sprints.post_processing: fal-ai/birefnet (remove-bg) for subjects, NOT for sprite-sheet
sprints.shared_style_stem: Cosmic Adventure 2026 hand-painted watercolor with ink underdrawing, paper-grain texture, faded-rose mineral wash + saffron-glow underlight + ink-aubergine ragged outline, Studio Ghibli x Moebius x Tenniel illustration style, oneiric dreamlike unsettling-fascinating mood

assets.notes: V1 (Flux Pro Ultra): photoreal ceramic mushroom, no body-horror — REJECTED (3/10). V2 (Flux Dev top-down): radial gill-pleats + central pucker + sickly faded-rose veins — ACCEPTED. Lesson: Flux Pro forces photo-aesthetic for "mushroom-cap" tokens, Flux Dev pliabler for stylized body-horror illustration.
STANDOUT first-pass: vertical-slit chameleon-iris with rings of saffron + faded-rose + pop-cyan, wet pearlescent sphere — unsettling sentient eyeball. Ultra worked perfectly here.

V1 (Flux Pro v1.1, single-image sprite-sheet): all 4 panels wide-open (symmetry-bias) — REJECTED. V2 (Flux Dev, single-image): still uniform mouths — REJECTED. V3 (4 separate flux-dev calls + PIL composite into 1024x512 sheet): closed/quarter/half/wide — WORKS. Lesson: diffusion can not be told to render sequential-animation states in one image, render each frame separately and composite deterministically.

V1 (Flux Pro Ultra): photoreal silver pocket-watch with one drip, no bubble enclosure — REJECTED. V2 (Flux Dev with WATERCOLOR PAINTING lead): watercolor clock with drip + softer iridescent bubble-enclosure feel — ACCEPTED. Bubble could be more orb-like but acceptable for game-scale.
First-pass success: pop-magenta + saffron crystal cluster with angular facets and prismatic refraction. Slight kawaii-tint but acceptable as hidden collectible.

V1 (Flux Pro Ultra): clipart-style flat blue cartoon star — REJECTED. V2 (Flux Dev WATERCOLOR PAINTING lead): better paper-grain + saffron core + pop-cyan body — ACCEPTED. Generic collectible, lowest weirdo of set — acceptable per brief.

V1 (Ultra): two-tree topology (lollipop with leafy crown above roots in middle) — REJECTED. V2 (Flux Dev "growing downward"): still two-tree — REJECTED. V3 (Flux Pro v1.1 "shape-not-orientation"): flame-bush with dripping roots — partial. V4 (Flux Dev THREE-ZONE prompt without word "tree"): TOP=root-fan + MIDDLE=trunk + BOTTOM=mossy-leaf-ball — ACCEPTED. Lesson: Flux "tree" sample-bias is unbreakable — must describe as 3-zone shape composition without using "tree" as a noun anchor.

STANDOUT first-pass: concentric pulsing aura-bands (saffron + magenta + cyan) ringing a window into mushroom-biome with star-rain visible. Hayao watercolor portal — perfect biome-switch object, "feels" like it breathes.

sprints.wiring_doc: ObjectManager preload via assetPath. Per-object spawn-config: { altitude, frequency, behaviour-class, audio-cue }. Sprint 15B handles actual ObjectManager wiring; this manifest provides the asset paths + per-object metadata.

sprints.sprint: Sprint 16A — Cosmo-LoRA fine-tune (DNA baked into model weights)
sprints.strategy: Train Flux LoRA on 10-image curated DNA-correct dataset, then generate hero with trigger word

sources: cosmo-walk-1, jump-up, cling (v053 originals — pose variation)
dataset.caption_format: rtcosmo, [pose-description], hayao moebius watercolor with paper grain, chameleon bulging spherical eyes glossy black with saffron catchlight, single antenna with faded rose flower bulb tip, two black flat suction cup discs at hand tips, faded rose spots on green moss-sage watercolor body, slim kid-frame proportions, no tail, slight overbite mouth, slightly uncute proportions, NOT kawaii NOT chibi NOT Disney

hero_generation.base_prompt_summary: rtcosmo standing pose facing camera + double anti-kawaii anti-Disney stack + DNA descriptors

post_processing.1_birefnet: fal-ai/birefnet model="General Use (Heavy)" operating_resolution=2048x2048 — removed peach moon halo cleanly
post_processing.2_chroma_cleanup: PIL chroma-key remaining peach pixels outside body silhouette
post_processing.3_tail_erase: PIL alpha-erase 2-region polygon (body-flank curve + curl-rectangle) — Sprint 6A pattern, 0% failure
post_processing.4_esrgan_4x: fal-ai/esrgan scale=4 → 6144² RGB (alpha preserved separately via Lanczos + threshold-tighten, Sprint 14A pattern)

sprints.why_this_works: Previous sprints (5B/6A/7A/11A/13D/14A) all failed to produce DNA-correct Cosmo from text-prompts because Flux training data biases dominated: kawaii eyes, lizard-tail, biped-fingers. PIL-paint workaround in 14A forced black eye-spheres deterministically but could not solve disc-hands (needed full anatomy regeneration). LoRA fine-tune bakes the DNA cluster (suction-cup discs, pearl-drop head, chameleon eyes, no-tail, antenna+bulb) directly into the model weights — so trigger word `rtcosmo` reliably evokes all DNA traits in a single generation. a03 is the FIRST organically-generated Cosmo with two clean black floating disc-pads — the architectural blocker identified across 6+ sprints is now resolved.

reuse_instructions.recommended_prompt_template: rtcosmo, [POSE], hayao moebius watercolor, NOT kawaii NOT chibi NOT Disney, chameleon bulging eye spheres, single antenna with faded rose flower bulb, two black flat suction cup discs at hand tips, no tail, faded rose spots on green moss-sage body, slim kid-frame, slightly uncute proportions
use_cases: Future hero re-renders at any size
Animation frame generation (walk/jump/cling) — no longer needs canonical-img2img which was architecturally blocked
Pose variation via direct text prompts (LoRA preserves DNA across poses)
Sprint 16B: 3D-Cosmo via Meshy v6 image-to-3D from this hero

sprints.sprint: Sprint 16B — 3D Cosmo rebuild from LoRA hero (DNA-correct mesh)
sprints.strategy: image-to-3D from Sprint 16A LoRA hero (cosmo-hero-lora.png) — DNA-correct mesh (no PIL-paint workaround)
sprints.pipeline: fal.ai upload → Meshy Studio /openapi/v1/image-to-3d (realistic, polycount=22000) → polled SUCCEEDED ~110s → GLB downloaded → gltf-transform optimize (draco+webp) for fallback
sprints.context: Sprint 15A cosmo.glb rendered off-brand 3D-blob-eyes because input had PIL-painted-eyes (2D painting interpreted as 3D depth). 16B uses the clean LoRA-rendered Cosmo (Sprint 16A 10/10 DNA) as image-to-3D input, yielding a mesh with eye geometry as native 3D bulging spheres.

sprints.rigging: NOT attempted — Sprint 15A confirmed Meshy auto-rig fails on alien anatomy. CosmoAgent uses procedural transforms per cosmo-animation-spec.json (15A pattern).

sprints.sprint: Sprint 17A — Cosmo 4 LoRA-poses + Blender handmade-rig + procedural-tilt-bones
sprints.strategy: Hybrid: Track A (4 LoRA static poses) + Track B (Blender rig on 16B base mesh) + Track C (rig-spec doc)

rigged_glb.pipeline: Blender 5.1 headless: import 16B mesh → 10-bone armature → distance-based weights → 4 NLA clips → GLB export

sprints.cost_usd_estimate: ~$1.00 (16 LoRA gens × ~0.05 + 4 BiRefNet + 4 ESRGAN; Blender local = $0)

sprints.theme: Composed worlds — layered parallax PNGs per biome
sprints.goal: 5-7 layered PNGs per biome (sky/distant/mid-A/mid-B/foreground/particle + optional creature for slow-bloom) for true 3D-feel parallax in companion-mode gyro-tilt.

biomes.description: bloom-pierced clouds, cosmic temple in sky

gotchas.flux_scene_magnet_on_cave_sky: Inkpool sky-gradient first-pass rendered an OUTDOOR sunset cumulus scene despite "deep underground cave-ceiling" prompt. Same scene-magnet bias as tile-prompts in Sprint 4.5 Fase B. Fix: 9-fold anti-outdoor stack ("NO sky NO outdoor NO sunset NO horizon NO clouds NO mountains NO landscape NO open-air NO atmosphere") + frame-the-perspective ("close-up underground cave-roof viewed from below, looking directly up at..."). Worked first try on regen.
gotchas.centered_clusters_despite_left_right_pro: Flux Pro Ultra centered the mushroom/crystal/gargoyle clusters in the canvas despite "occupies the LEFT/RIGHT third leaving other side as empty void" prompts. Workaround: composition-spec uses x_offset normalized [-1,1] to anchor BiRefNet'd layers to LEFT (-0.25) and RIGHT (+0.25) thirds at composite-time. Cleaner than forcing Flux composition.
gotchas.birefnet_heavy_on_jet_black: BiRefNet HEAVY @ 2048² works flawlessly on jet-black-void backgrounds painted by Flux. 17/17 successful first try, no soft-edge halo loss. Crucial: prompt for "isolated cluster on pure flat solid jet-black void background" upfront → Flux paints clean cutout, BiRefNet extracts cleanly.

sprints.wiring_pending_17B_main_ts: BiomeManager needs compositionSpec: BiomeCompositionSpec field with layers[] from each biome's composition-spec.json. Three.js parallaxScene must load N planes per biome and apply MotionController-driven camera pan effective-X to layer.position.x with parallax_multiplier scaling. See composition-spec.json parallax_notes for implementation details.

sprints.strategy: Deterministic crop from Sprint 16A LoRA-locked hero (cosmo-hero-lora.png 4096² 10/10 DNA). Pivoted away from Flux-LoRA-organ-prompt after 3-attempt observation that LoRA at scale 1.0 summons full character even with isolation prompts.
sprints.pipeline: PIL crop → soft-elliptical alpha mask → 1024² resize. Body-skin uses 4-quadrant tile-blend.
